close.description: Closes a direct message or multi-person direct message

create.description: Initiates a public or private channel-based conversation

get.description: Get the reactions of a message

getAll.description: Get a list of many users

history.description: Get a conversation's history of messages and events

invite.description: Invite a user to a channel

kick.description: Removes a user from a channel

open.description: Opens or resumes a direct message or multi-person direct message

replies.description: Get a thread of messages posted to a channel

setPurpose.description: Sets the purpose for a conversation

setTopic.description: Sets the topic for a conversation

delete.description: Delete a star from an item

add.description: Adds a reaction to a message

upload.description: Create or upload an existing file

remove.description: Remove a reaction of a message

getPresence.description: Get online status of a user

channelId.description: The Slack channel to get the reactions from

channelVisibility.description: Whether to create a Public or a Private Slack channel. More info.

userIds.description: The ID of the user to invite into channel. Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression.

channelId.description: The channel to send the file to. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

returnIm.description: Whether you want the full IM channel definition in the response

users.description: If only one user is included, this creates a 1:1 DM. The ordering of the users is preserved whenever a multi-person direct message is returned. Supply a channel when not supplying users. Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression.

searchChannel.description: Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression

limitWaitTime.description: Whether to limit the time this node should wait for a user response before execution resumes

limitType.description: Sets the condition for the execution to resume. Can be a specified date or after some time.

afterTimeInterval.description: Waits for a certain amount of time

atSpecifiedTime.description: Waits until the set date and time to continue

maxDateAndTime.description: Continue execution after the specified date and time

limitType.description: Sets the condition for the execution to resume. Can be a specified date or after some time.

afterTimeInterval.description: Waits for a certain amount of time

atSpecifiedTime.description: Waits until the set date and time to continue

maxDateAndTime.description: Continue execution after the specified date and time

appendAttribution.description: Whether to include the phrase "This message was sent automatically with n8n" to the end of the message

thread_ts.description: Provide another message's Timestamp value to make this message a reply
Message timestamps are included in output data of Slack nodes, abbreviated to ts

reply_broadcast.description: Whether the reply should be made visible to everyone in the channel or conversation

thread_ts.description: Message timestamps are included in output data of Slack nodes, abbreviated to ts

reply_broadcast.description: Whether the reply should be made visible to everyone in the channel or conversation

responseFormTitle.description: Title of the form that the user can access to provide their response

responseFormDescription.description: Description of the form that the user can access to provide their response

responseFormCustomCss.description: Override default styling of the response form with CSS

fileComment.description: File comment to add star to

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the message to delete

channelIds.description: The channels to send the file to. Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression.

initialComment.description: The message text introducing the file in specified channels

threadTs.description: Provide another message's Timestamp value to upload this file as a reply. Never use a reply's Timestamp value; use its parent instead.

id.description: ID of the field to set. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

value.description: Value of the field to set

id.description: ID of the field to set. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

value.description: Value of the field to set

email.description: This field can only be changed by admins for users on paid teams

status_emoji.description: Is a string referencing an emoji enabled for the Slack team, such as :mountain_railway:

status_expiration.description: The number of minutes to wait until this status expires and is cleared. Optional.

status_text.description: Allows up to 100 characters, though we strongly encourage brevity

status_emoji.description: Is a string referencing an emoji enabled for the Slack team, such as :mountain_railway:

status_expiration.description: The number of minutes to wait until this status expires and is cleared. Optional.

status_text.description: Allows up to 100 characters, though we strongly encourage brevity

user.description: ID of user to change. This argument may only be specified by team admins on paid teams.

include_count.description: Whether to include the number of users in the User Group

include_disabled.description: Whether to include disabled User Groups

include_users.description: Whether to include the list of users for each User Group

resolveData.description: Whether to return full user objects instead of just user IDs

userId.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

returnAll.description: Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit

limit.description: Max number of results to return

excludeArchived.description: Whether to exclude archived channels from the list

inclusive.description: Whether to include messages with latest or oldest timestamp in results only when either timestamp is specified

latest.description: End of time range of messages to include in results

oldest.description: Start of time range of messages to include in results

channelId.description: Channel containing the file to be listed. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

showFilesHidden.description: Whether to show truncated file info for files hidden due to being too old, and the team who owns the file being over the file limit

tsFrom.description: Filter files created after this timestamp (inclusive)

tsTo.description: Filter files created before this timestamp (inclusive)

userId.description: Filter files created by a single user. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

resolveData.description: Whether to resolve the data automatically. By default the response only contain the ID to resource.

name.description: A name for the User Group. Must be unique among User Groups.

ts.description: Timestamp of the message to update

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the message to add, get or remove

user.description: The ID of the user to get the online status of

messageType.description: Whether to send a simple text message, or use Slack’s Blocks UI builder for more sophisticated messages that include form fields, sections and more

block.description: Combine text, buttons, form elements, dividers and more in Slack 's visual builder

text.description: The message text to update. Supports markdown by default - this can be disabled in "Options".

blocksUi.description: Enter the JSON output from Slack's visual Block Kit Builder here. You can then use expressions to add variable content to your blocks. To create blocks, use Slack's Block Kit Builder

noticeAttachments.displayName: This is a legacy Slack feature. Slack advises to instead use Blocks.

fallback.description: Required plain-text summary of the attachment

color.description: Color of the line left of text

pretext.description: Text which appears before the message block

author_icon.description: Icon which should appear for the user

footer_icon.description: Icon which should appear next to footer

ts.description: Timestamp of the message to post

short.description: Whether items can be displayed next to each other
Whether items can be displayed next to each other

includeLinkToWorkflow.description: Whether to append a link to this workflow at the end of the message. This is helpful if you have many workflows sending Slack messages.

botProfile.description: Set an image or an emoji as the Profile Photo (avatar) of the bot sending the message. Will not be used if sending message as a user.

icon_emoji.description: Only used if sending message as a bot. Use emoji codes like +1, not an actual emoji like 👍. List of common emoji codes

icon_url.description: Only used if sending message as a bot

icon_emoji.description: Only used if sending message as a bot. Use emoji codes like +1, not an actual emoji like 👍. List of common emoji codes

icon_url.description: Only used if sending message as a bot

link_names.description: Whether to turn @users and #channels in message text into clickable links

thread_ts.description: Provide another message's Timestamp value to make this message a reply
Message timestamps are included in output data of Slack nodes, abbreviated to ts

reply_broadcast.description: Whether the reply should be made visible to everyone in the channel or conversation

thread_ts.description: Message timestamps are included in output data of Slack nodes, abbreviated to ts

reply_broadcast.description: Whether the reply should be made visible to everyone in the channel or conversation

mrkdwn.description: Whether to use Slack Markdown to format the message

unfurl_links.description: Whether to enable unfurling of primarily text-based content

unfurl_media.description: Whether to disable unfurling of media content

ephemeral.description: Whether to send a temporary, ephemeral message

sendAsUser.description: The message will be sent from this username (i.e. as if this individual sent the message). Add chat:write.customize scope on Slack API

link_names.description: Whether to find and link channel names and usernames

channels.description: A comma-separated string of encoded channel IDs for which the User Group uses as a default. Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression.

description.description: A short description of the User Group

handle.description: A mention handle. Must be unique among channels, users and User Groups.

include_count.description: Whether to include the number of users in each User Group

name.description: A name for the User Group. Must be unique among User Groups.

query.description: The text to search for within messages

sort.description: How search results should be sorted. You can sort by.

approval.description: User can approve/disapprove from within the message

freeText.description: User can submit a response via a form

customForm.description: User can submit a response via a custom form

fieldName.description: The name of the field, used in input attributes and referenced by the workflow

fieldLabel.description: Label that appears above the input field

fieldType.description: The type of field to add to the form

elementName.description: Optional field. It can be used to include the html in the output.

placeholder.description: Sample text to display inside the field

defaultValue.description: Default value(s) that will be pre-selected. Must match one or multiple of the option labels. Separate multiple pre-selected options with a comma.

fieldValue.description: Input value can be set here or will be passed as a query parameter via Field Name if no value is set

multiselectLegacyNotice.displayName: Multiple Choice is a legacy option, please use Checkboxes or Radio Buttons field type instead

multiselect.description: Whether to allow the user to select multiple options from the dropdown list

html.description: HTML elements to display on the form page

multipleFiles.description: Whether to allow the user to select multiple files from the file input or just one

acceptFileTypes.description: Comma-separated list of allowed file extensions

formatDate.displayName: The displayed date is formatted based on the locale of the user's browser

requiredField.description: Whether to require the user to enter a value for this field before submitting the form

binaryData.description: Whether the data to upload should be taken from binary field
